question_text: A gas in a rigid container has a pressure of 200 Pa at room temperature (20°C). If the temperature is increased to 40°C, what is the approximate new pressure? (Assume the volume remains constant.)
correct_answer: Approximately 207 Pa (or 206-208 Pa acceptable). Using P₁/T₁ = P₂/T₂: 200/293 = P₂/313, so P₂ = 200 × (313/293) ≈ 207 Pa
explanation: When volume is constant, pressure is directly proportional to absolute temperature. Convert temperatures to Kelvin (20°C = 293 K, 40°C = 313 K) and use the pressure-temperature relationship to find the new pressure.
